verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 lacks a transmembrane domain, distinguishing it from the other IZUMO family members (IZUMO1/2/3). In sperm it is localized as an intraacrosomal protein, and in somatic tissues Human Protein Atlas and GeneCards annotations describe cytoplasmic and nuclear localization respectively. It is lost from sperm after the acrosome reaction rather than appearing on the surface. No GPI anchor, outer-leaflet lipidation, or stable TM-partner-anchored surface pool is documented. No surface-directed therapeutic programs exist for this protein. Pre-no checklist: no cancer ecto-pool, no cell-death surface display, no activation-induced surface display documented.
